action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 failed with TypeError because model.py's render_views expected the internal renderer to return a dict, but it returned a torch.Tensor. This discrepancy with the earlier successful solve_render.py suggests the output format may depend on subtle input-shape or environment factors, or the prior successful run might have used a slightly different call path. Analysis: Instead of debugging the exact cause of the tensor return, I will rewrite model.py with robust output handling that accepts both dict (rgb+opacity / comp_rgb) and Tensor outputs, reshaping them into [H,W,4] RGBA images. I will also make checkpoint loading robust to both wrapper-level and model-level state dicts. Decision: 1) Write clean model.py with robust rendering and checkpoint logic. 2) Save the pretrained TripoSR weights as checkpoints/baseline.pt. 3) Run a fast single-object smoke test. 4) If it passes, immediately submit eval to get the baseline LPIPS score and iteration time.
action.command:
mkdir -p checkpoints && python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr'); from model import TripoSRWrapper; import torch; w = TripoSRWrapper(device='cuda'); torch.save(w.state_dict(), 'checkpoints/baseline.pt'); print('Saved checkpoints/baseline.pt')" && python -c "
import sys, os, json
sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr')
from PIL import Image
from model import TripoSRWrapper

device='cuda'
w = TripoSRWrapper(device=device, checkpoint_path='checkpoints/baseline.pt')
data_dir = os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR', '/data')
obj_id = sorted(os.listdir(os.path.join(data_dir, 'val')))[0]
obj_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, 'val', obj_id)
print('Smoke test on', obj_id)
img = Image.open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'input.png'))
sc = w.encode(img)
with open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'cameras.json')) as f:
    cams = json.load(f)
renders = w.render_views(sc, cams[1:])
print('Success:', len(renders), 'renders, first shape', renders[0].shape, 'dtype', renders[0].dtype)
Image.fromarray(renders[0], mode='RGBA').save('test_render.png')
print('Saved test_render.png')
"
